methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Anthrogen is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Ankit Singhal that has raised $4.5M to date. The valuation landscape includes 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, including companies such as Xaira Therapeutics, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression proved applicable. The peer-median approach failed due to insufficient narrow-set comparables—while 14 same-sector peers exist, none of the filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier) yielded the required minimum of three datapoints. Public-comp anchoring was inapplicable because Anthrogen's revenue is not disclosed, which is typical for private companies. The stage-progression method applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $4.5M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $11.3M.

The analysis supports a range of $9.6M to $13M, with a median of $11M. This range reflects the single-method triangulation; robustness is constrained by data limitations rather than methodological disagreement. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data (which would enable public-comp anchoring) and the thinness of comparable seed-stage biotech exits in the dataset. The stage-progression result should be treated as indicative rather than definitive pending disclosure of additional operational or financial metrics.
